name: accessibility
context: Web accessibility (a11y): ARIA, semantic HTML, keyboard navigation, screen readers, WCAG compliance
info: Accessibility ensures web applications are usable by everyone, including people using screen readers, keyboard-only navigation, and assistive technologies. This guide covers semantic HTML as the foundation, ARIA roles and attributes for dynamic widgets, keyboard navigation patterns, testing tools for automated and manual audits, and a WCAG 2.1 AA compliance checklist. Accessibility is not an afterthought — it should be built into every component from the start. All [[frontend/react]] components should follow these patterns. Styles in [[frontend/css]] must meet color contrast and motion requirements.
body:
# Web Accessibility (a11y) Accessibility means building web experiences that work for everyone — people using screen readers, keyboard-only users, users with low vision, motor impairments, or cognitive disabilities. The target standard is WCAG 2.1 Level AA. ## Semantic HTML Semantic HTML is the foundation of accessibility. Native elements carry built-in roles, keyboard behavior, and screen reader announcements: ```tsx {/* Bad: div soup */}
Click me
{/* Good: semantic elements */} {/* Bad: generic container */}
...
{/* Good: landmark elements */}
  • Dashboard
  • Settings
``` | Element | Built-in Role | Use For | |---------|---------------|---------| | ` ) : null; } ``` Focus management rules: - Trap focus inside modals and dialogs - Return focus to the trigger element on close - Visible focus indicators on all interactive elements (see [[frontend/css#tailwind]] `ring-*` utilities) - Skip navigation links for keyboard users ## Testing Tools | Tool | Type | Use | |------|------|-----| | axe-core / @axe-core/react | Automated | Catch common ARIA and contrast issues | | eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y | Linting | Catch issues at code time | | Playwright + axe | E2E | Automated accessibility in CI — see [[frontend/testing#accessibility-testing]] | | VoiceOver (macOS) | Manual | Screen reader testing | | NVDA (Windows) | Manual | Screen reader testing | | Chrome DevTools Accessibility | Manual | Inspect accessibility tree | ## WCAG 2.1 AA Checklist - [ ] All images have `alt` text (or `alt=""` for decorative) - [ ] Color contrast ratio: 4.5:1 for normal text, 3:1 for large text (see [[frontend/css#tailwind]]) - [ ] All interactive elements are keyboard accessible - [ ] Focus is visible on all interactive elements - [ ] Page has a logical heading hierarchy (`h1` > `h2` > `h3`) - [ ] Forms have associated labels (`

sections.title: Semantic HTML
sections.body: Use native HTML elements that carry built-in roles and keyboard behavior: button, a, nav, main, aside, header, footer, h1-h6, ul/ol. Semantic elements are announced correctly by screen readers and receive keyboard focus automatically. Avoid div/span for interactive elements. See [[frontend/react#components-jsx]] for component patterns.

sections.title: ARIA roles and attributes
sections.body: First rule: don't use ARIA if a native element works. aria-label and aria-labelledby for accessible names. aria-describedby for help text and errors. aria-expanded for disclosure widgets. aria-live for dynamic announcements. aria-hidden for decorative content. WAI-ARIA Authoring Practices provide patterns for tabs, menus, comboboxes, dialogs.

sections.title: Keyboard navigation
sections.body: Tab for focus order, Enter/Space to activate, Escape to close, Arrow keys within composite widgets. Trap focus in modals. Return focus on close. Visible focus indicators required — use Tailwind `focus-visible:ring-*` utilities (see [[frontend/css#tailwind]]). Skip navigation links for page structure. tabIndex={-1} for programmatic focus, tabIndex={0} for natural tab order.

sections.title: Accessibility testing tools
sections.body: axe-core for automated ARIA and contrast checks. eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y for lint-time issues. Playwright + axe for CI automation — see [[frontend/testing#accessibility-testing]]. VoiceOver (macOS) and NVDA (Windows) for manual screen reader testing. Chrome DevTools accessibility panel for inspecting the accessibility tree.

sections.title: WCAG 2.1 AA checklist
sections.body: Alt text on images, 4.5:1 color contrast for normal text, keyboard-accessible interactions, visible focus indicators, logical heading hierarchy, labeled form inputs, error messages linked via aria-describedby, aria-live for dynamic content, prefers-reduced-motion for animations (see [[frontend/css#animations]]), 44x44px touch targets, html lang attribute, skip navigation link.

links.context: Component patterns that should be accessible by default

related.note: All React components should follow accessibility patterns
CSS must meet color contrast and motion requirements
Accessibility testing should be automated in CI

author: dojo-community
